message.content:
Thoroughness: very thorough

Explore the modular apartment planner codebase to understand the UI components, toolbar, and sidebar. I need to understand:

1. Read files in `src/ui/` - understand Toolbar, Sidebar, PropertiesPanel, Modal, and any other UI components. What's the UI layout pattern?

2. Look for any existing panel/tab system in the sidebar - is there a floor selector? Layer panel? This would inform where the phase panel goes.

3. Read `src/ui/PropertiesPanel` - how are object properties displayed and edited? This matters for adding phaseId to the properties panel.

4. Look for any existing dropdown/select components that could be reused for phase selection

5. Check for any CSS module patterns used across the UI

6. Look for how the floor/level selector UI works (if any) - as a pattern for the phase selector

7. Check `src/ui/Sidebar` in detail - what sections does it have? How is it organized?

Report back with:
- Complete UI component inventory
- Sidebar structure and sections
- PropertiesPanel structure and how properties are edited
- Any reusable form components (dropdowns, inputs, color pickers)
- Patterns for adding new UI panels/sections
- How floor selection UI works (if exists)

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough exploration of the UI components, sidebar, and related systems. Let me start by examining the files in the `src/ui/` directory.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the UI architecture. Let me create a detailed report. ## Complete UI Component Inventory & Architecture Analysis Based on my thorough exploration of the modular apartment planner codebase, here's the comprehensive report: ### 1. UI Component Inventory **Main Components in `src/ui/`:** - `Toolbar.jsx` - Horizontal toolbar at top with file operations, view modes, tool palette, fixtures, and panel toggles - `Sidebar.jsx` - Left sidebar with collapsible sections for project hierarchy - `PropertiesPanel.jsx` - Right sidebar panel (84.2 KB file!) with property editors for selected objects - `InputField.jsx` - Reusable form input component - `Modal.jsx` - Dialog overlay for modals - `ToolbarIcons.jsx` - SVG icon definitions - `CompassOverlay.jsx` - Navigation compass display **Associated CSS Modules:** - `Toolbar.module.css` - 314 lines - `Sidebar.module.css` - 275 lines - `PropertiesPanel.module.css` - 200+ lines - `InputField.module.css` - 55 lines - `Modal.module.css` - 118 lines ### 2. Overall UI Layout Pattern The app uses a **3-column grid layout** defined in `App.module.css`: ``` [Sidebar (280px)] [Canvas/Content (1fr)] [PropertiesPanel (320px)] [Toolbar spans all columns at top] ``` Layout is CSS Grid-based with: - `--sidebar-width` CSS variable (280px) - `--properties-width` CSS variable (320px) - Both panels can be hidden via `panelHidden` CSS class - Toolbar height: `--toolbar-height` (assumed 56-60px) ### 3. Sidebar Structure & Organization **Sidebar.jsx** has a hierarchical collapsible section pattern: **Static Sections (always visible):** - **Project** - Single input field for project name **Collapsible Sections (state tracked in `collapsedSections` object):** - **Floors** (with +Add button for new floors) - Floor cards (individual floor entries with meta info: level index, elevation) - Each floor has duplicate/delete action buttons - Shows: Floor name + "L{index} · {elevation} mm" - **Sheets** (with +Add button) - Sheet list items **Floor-Context Sections** (only visible when a floor is active - sticky header "Floor: {name}"): - **Slabs** - Slab objects - **Walls** - Wall objects - **Annotations** - Dimension annotations - **Beams** - Beam objects - **Stairs** - Stair objects - **Landings** - Landing objects - **Doors** - Door objects - **Windows** - Window objects - **Columns** - Column objects - **Rooms** - Room objects **Key UI Patterns:** - `Section` component wraps each section with: - Chevron icon (rotates on collapse) - Title (uppercase, 11px font) - Count badge (item count in top-right) - Optional action button (+) - Items show `.itemSelected` state with accent background + left border highlight - Floor cards are larger components with dual-column layout (button + action icons) ### 4. PropertiesPanel Structure & Property Editing **PropertiesPanel.jsx** (1831 lines) - A massive conditional render system: **Content Selection Pattern:** The panel uses a **type-based content routing** system: ```javascript let content = /* default content based on workspaceMode */; if (selectedId && workspaceMode === 'sheet' && sheet) { // Route to sheet-related editors if (selectedType === 'sheet') content = ; if (selectedType === 'sheetViewport') content = ; } else if (selectedId && floor) { // Route to floor object editors if (selectedType === 'floor') content = ; if (selectedType === 'slab') content = ; if (selectedType === 'wall') content = ; // ... 15+ more types } ``` **Default Content (when nothing selected):** - `` - Floor editor (level, height, etc.) - `` - Project-wide settings **Default Content (sheet mode):** - `` - Sheet metadata and layout editor **Property Editor Components (all follow same pattern):** 1. `WallProperties` - Coordinates, thickness, height 2. `DoorProperties` - Type select dropdown, dimensions, swing direction toggle 3. `WindowProperties` - Type select dropdown, dimensions, sill height, offset 4. `RoomProperties` - Name, area (read-only), **color picker** + hex input 5. `ColumnProperties` - Name, position (X/Y), dimensions (W/D/H), label visibility checkbox 6. `SlabProperties` - Name, type, thickness, elevation, area (calculated), label 7. `BeamProperties` - Start/end refs (read-only), width, depth, floor level 8. `AnnotationProperties` - Mode dropdown, coordinates, offset, text override, measured value 9. `StairProperties` - Similar pattern 10. `LandingProperties` - Similar pattern 11. `FixtureProperties` - Similar pattern 12. `RailingProperties` - Similar pattern 13. `SheetProperties` - Sheet metadata, layout template, title block, viewports 14. `SheetViewportProperties` - Viewport editing **Panel Wrapper (`.panel`):** - Background: `--color-panel-bg` - Border-left: divider line - Overflow-y: auto with thin scrollbar - Padding: `--space-lg` - Flex column with gap ### 5. Form Component Patterns & Reusable Components **InputField Component:** ```jsx ``` **Styling Pattern for InputField:** ```css .field { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 6px; } .label { flex: 0 0 80px; font-size: 12px; } /* Fixed width label column */ .input { flex: 1; height: 28px; padding: 0 8px; } .inputReadonly { background: --color-panel-bg-alt; opacity reduced } .suffix { font-size: 10px; color: tertiary; font-family: var(--font-blueprint); } ``` **Custom Select/Dropdown Pattern:** Inline ` ``` **Color Picker Pattern (RoomProperties):** - `.colorField` - flex container - `.colorLabel` - 80px fixed width - `.colorControls` - flex row with: - `.colorPicker` - `` (44x32px) - `.colorHexInput` - Text input for #RRGGBB (72px wide) **Units Toggle (Segmented Control):** ```jsx
``` **Custom Checkboxes (inline layout):** ```jsx
``` **Action Buttons:** - `.actionBtn` - Accent ghost button (accent color text on transparent background) - `.deleteBtn` - Danger ghost button (red text, red border, red hover bg) - Styling via `var(--color-danger)`, `var(--color-danger-ghost-border)`, etc. ### 6. CSS Module Patterns Used Across UI **Design Tokens (CSS Custom Properties):** ```css --toolbar-height --sidebar-width (280px) --properties-width (320px) --color-panel-bg --color-border --color-text-primary / secondary / tertiary --color-accent (teal: #0d7c66) --color-accent-subtle (rgba tint) --color-segment-bg / active-bg / active-shadow --color-surface-elevated --color-divider --color-danger / danger-ghost-border / danger-ghost-bg-hover --space-xs / sm / md / lg / xl --radius-sm / md / lg --duration-fast --font-blueprint (monospace for numbers) --shadow-overlay ``` **Composition Pattern (CSS Modules):** - Uses `composes` for class inheritance: ```css .segmentBtnActive { composes: segmentBtn; /* override some props */ } ``` **Standard Button Sizes:** - Toolbar buttons: 36x36px - Toolbar tool palette: 34x34px - Tool palette icons: 18x18px - Floor action buttons: 26x26px - Input fields: 28px height - Segmented buttons: 30px height, 10px horizontal padding **Interaction Patterns:** - Hover: `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)` background shift + color brightening - Active: `scale(0.97)` transform for tactile feedback - Focus (inputs): Accent border color + accent subtle shadow - Disabled: `opacity: 0.35` + `cursor: not-allowed` ### 7. Toolbar Architecture **Toolbar.jsx** groups buttons into sections: 1. **File & Clipboard** group: - New, Undo, Redo - Save (with dirty state indicator pulse) - Load - Copy, Cut, Paste 2. **Segmented View Modes** (pill-shaped): - Plan, Front, Rear, Left, Right, Section, Sheets - Only one active at a time - CSS styling: pill-shaped with inner active state 3. **Tool Palette** (inline row): - Select, Dimension, Wall, Beam, Stair, Section, Slab, Room, Door, Window, Column, Landing, Railing - 12 tools, shows active tool in accent color - Disabled when not in plan view 4. **Fixture Palette** (inline row): - 7 fixtures (Kitchen Top, Toilet, Lavatory, Table, TV, Sofa, Bed) - Same activation pattern as tools 5. **Panel Toggles & Utilities**: - Sidebar toggle (state: active/inactive opacity) - Properties toggle - Grid toggle - Snap toggle - Detect Rooms button **Toolbar CSS:** - Horizontal flex layout with `overflow-x: auto` (scrollable on small screens) - Groups wrapped in `.group` containers with rounded backgrounds - Dividers between groups (1px vertical line) - Spacer at end to push content left - Responsive breakpoints hide labels on smaller screens ### 8. Floor Selection UI (Pattern for Phase Panel) **No dedicated floor selector UI exists** - floor selection happens via: 1. **Sidebar floor card** - Click to select and activate floor 2. **Floor card styling** - `.floorCard` + `.floorCardActive` for current selection 3. **Sticky context header** - "Floor: {name}" shows which floor you're working on 4. **Properties panel** - Shows floor-specific sections below **Floor Card Structure:** ```jsx
{/* Duplicate & Delete buttons */}
``` ### 9. Tab/Panel System Patterns **No dedicated tab component exists** - the app uses: - **Collapsible sections** with chevron icons and state management - **Conditional rendering** based on `selectedType` in PropertiesPanel - **Sticky headers** for context (e.g., "Floor: {name}") - **Segmented controls** for mutually-exclusive options (view modes, units) ### 10. Key Takeaways for Phase Panel Implementation **Pattern to Follow:** 1. **Use sidebar section pattern** - Create a "Phases" section in Sidebar with collapsible header 2. **Use Properties panel dropdown pattern** - Add a phase select dropdown to object property editors 3. **Use segmented control or dropdown** - For switching between phases (similar to units toggle in PropertiesPanel) 4. **Use InputField for phase properties** - Text inputs for phase name, color, etc. 5. **Use color picker pattern** - For phase visualization colors (like rooms) 6. **Use action button pattern** - For add/duplicate/delete phase actions 7. **CSS Module reuse** - All styling uses design tokens defined in CSS variables 8. **No specialized components needed** - Everything can be built from existing: - InputField (for text/number inputs) - Native `` (for color picker) - `.actionBtn` / `.deleteBtn` pattern (for phase actions) **File Paths for Reference:** - `/src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` - Sidebar section pattern - `/src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` - Property editor pattern & content routing - `/src/ui/InputField.jsx` - Form input component - `/src/ui/InputField.module.css` - Input styling patterns - `/src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` - Section & list item styling - `/src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` - Panel layout & typography - `/src/app/App.module.css` - Overall grid layout
